Title: Multi-omics analyses reveal regulatory networks underpinning metabolite biosynthesis in Nicotiana tabacum

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GMRN correlation; RNA-seq/RT-qPCR in NtMYB28 overexpression and knockout lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly consistent with hydroxycinnamic acid accumulation across leaf development after topping
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, providing entry into phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher NtPAL2 expression is associated with increased hydroxycinnamic acids including chlorogenic acid; reduced in ntmyb28 knockout
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants across two ecological regions; developmental series after topping; cold stress discussed for regulator but not direct PAL2 manipulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant; anti-inflammatory; antiviral; hypoglycemic; hypolipidemic; contributes to stress protection
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: phenylpropanoid entry step leading to hydroxycinnamic acid/CGA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful structural target to increase chlorogenic acid when combined with NtMYB28
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtPAL2 was identified by the GMRN as a top structural gene associated with chlorogenic acid accumulation. 2. NtMYB28 directly binds and activates the NtPAL2 promoter. 3. NtPAL2 transcript levels increase in NtMYB28-OE and decrease in ntmyb28-KO plants, matching hydroxycinnamic acid changes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper refers to PAL and 4CL as rate-limiting enzymes for CGA biosynthesis; direct transgenic manipulation was on NtMYB28, not NtPAL2 alone.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA1112100, PRJNA1112565; metabolomics: MTBLS13081, MTBLS13060

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GMRN correlation; promoter binding by Y1H/EMSA/LUC via NtMYB28; RNA-seq/RT-qPCR in NtMYB28 overexpression and knockout lines
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly consistent with hydroxycinnamic acid accumulation across leaf development after topping
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: activates hydroxycinnamic acids such as coumarate/caffeate to their CoA thioesters for downstream chlorogenic acid formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher Nt4CL2A expression is associated with increased hydroxycinnamic acids including chlorogenic acid; reduced in ntmyb28 knockout
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants across two ecological regions; developmental series after topping
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant; anti-inflammatory; antiviral; hypoglycemic; hypolipidemic; contributes to stress protection
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: hydroxycinnamic acids plus CoA and ATP
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful structural target to increase chlorogenic acid when combined with NtMYB28
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Nt4CL2A was identified by the GMRN as a top structural gene associated with chlorogenic acid accumulation. 2. NtMYB28 directly binds and activates the Nt4CL2A promoter through an AC-I motif. 3. Nt4CL2A expression rises in NtMYB28-OE and falls in ntmyb28-KO plants, paralleling hydroxycinnamic acid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The article text once mislabels Nt4CL2A as encoding cinnamate 4-hydroxylase, but elsewhere and by gene symbol/context it is clearly 4CL2A.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA1112100, PRJNA1112565; metabolomics: MTBLS13081, MTBLS13060

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GMRN correlation; Y1H/EMSA/dual-luciferase for NtERF167 promoter binding; RNA-seq/RT-qPCR in NtERF167 overexpression and knockout lines; lipidomics
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly correlated with NtERF167 and lipid accumulation during leaf development
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies activated fatty acyl-CoA pool for membrane/storage lipid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: activates long-chain fatty acids to acyl-CoA thioesters for downstream glycerolipid and cuticular lipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive association with increased total lipids, glycerophospholipids, DAG and TAG under NtERF167 activation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants across two ecological regions; developmental series after topping; cold stress context for regulator
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: membrane integrity; stress resistance; biofuel feedstock
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: fatty acid activation linking chloroplast and ER lipid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target for boosting leaf oil/lipid production, especially with NtERF167
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtLACS2 was prioritized by the GMRN as the strongest structural target of NtERF167 in lipid metabolism. 2. NtERF167 directly binds and activates the NtLACS2 promoter. 3. NtLACS2 expression is elevated in NtERF167-OE and reduced in nterf167-KO plants, accompanying lipid accumulation changes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a bridge between chloroplast and endoplasmic reticulum lipid synthesis pathways.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GMRN correlation; Y1H/EMSA/dual-luciferase for NtCYC promoter binding; RNA-seq/RT-qPCR in NtCYC overexpression and knockout lines; aroma metabolite profiling; MDA assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly correlated with NtCYC and DHA during leaf development
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux redirection decreased β-damascenone while increasing DHA and β-ionone; associated with increased lipid peroxidation/ROS-related MDA when activated by NtCYC
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: drives flux toward target product through carotenoid co-oxidative degradation during fatty acid oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids such as linoleic and linolenic acids, generating radicals that promote co-oxidative carotenoid cleavage to aroma volatiles including DHA and β-ionone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased NtLOX2 expression promotes DHA and β-ionone accumulation; altered aroma flux and reduced β-damascenone
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants across two ecological regions; developmental series after topping; cold stress context for regulator
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: aroma/flavor compound; fragrance ingredient; stress signaling molecule
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: fatty acid hydroperoxides; indirect apocarotenoid volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: carotenoid degradation coupled with fatty acid oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key target for engineering tobacco aroma, but altered oxidative balance and branch flux should be monitored
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtLOX2 showed the strongest association with DHA in the GMRN. 2. NtCYC directly binds and activates the NtLOX2 promoter. 3. NtLOX2 upregulation in NtCYC-OE plants increases DHA and β-ionone while shifting flux away from β-damascenone.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper interprets LOX-mediated free-radical chemistry as enabling nonspecific carotenoid cleavage; also notes involvement of LOX genes in jasmonate-related pathways.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression pattern highly consistent with phenylpropanoid biosynthetic genes and hydroxycinnamic acid accumulation during leaf development
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antioxidant; anti-inflammatory; antiviral; hypoglycemic; hypolipidemic; contributes to abiotic stress protection
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: development after topping; ecological differences between low-altitude and high-altitude field sites; cold stress context examined
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key positive regulator of hydroxycinnamic acid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding to AC-I motif
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering tobacco with elevated chlorogenic acid and improved cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to an R2R3-MYB context by discussion, but exact subclass beyond MYB not explicitly stated in Results.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA1112100, PRJNA1112565; metabolomics: MTBLS13081, MTBLS13060
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GMRN identified NtMYB28 as the strongest TF associated with chlorogenic acid biosynthesis. 2. NtMYB28 directly binds the Nt4CL2A and NtPAL2 promoters and activates their transcription. 3. NtMYB28 overexpression increases hydroxycinnamic acids by 1.2-2.0 fold, whereas knockout reduces them by 1.5-2.3 fold. 4. NtMYB28 also enhances cold-stress tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for overexpression; native promoters of Nt4CL2A and NtPAL2 assayed in transient reporter system
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major growth penalty reported; alters transcriptome broadly; beneficial for cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants in high-altitude vs low-altitude regions; developmental time course after topping; cold stress at 10°C

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; lipidomics
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly correlated with lipid biosynthesis genes and lipid accumulation during leaf development
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: membrane stability; stress resistance; storage lipids and biofuel feedstock
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: development after topping; ecological field differences; cold stress context evaluated
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key positive regulator of lipid metabolism and lipidomic flux
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via direct binding to GCC-box in promoter
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for increasing tobacco leaf oil/lipid content and improving cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetically related to NtERF1 and NtSHN2; genes/metabolites co-regulated with NtERF167 also enriched in flavonoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtERF167 was identified from the ERF-centered GMRN as an uncharacterized high-connectivity lipid regulator. 2. NtERF167 directly binds and activates the NtLACS2 promoter. 3. NtERF167 overexpression increases total lipids, glycerophospholipids, DAG and TAG, whereas knockout decreases them. 4. NtERF167 contributes to cold-stress tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for overexpression; native promoter of NtLACS2 assayed in transient reporter system
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis; precursor activation; lipid flux rewiring
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad lipidomic rewiring; no yield penalty reported; loss of function increases cold sensitivity
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants in high-altitude vs low-altitude regions; developmental time course after topping; cold stress at 10°C

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; aroma metabolite profiling; MDA measurement
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly correlated with NtLOX2 and DHA during leaf development
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fragrance/flavor compound and plant stress signaling molecule
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: development after topping; ecological field differences; cold stress context evaluated
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key regulator driving aroma formation through metabolic flux redirection
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering tobacco aroma compounds such as DHA and β-ionone, but oxidative stress and cold-tolerance penalties must be considered
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetically close to TCP4; influences terpenoid and JA-related network genes; overexpression raises MDA and knockout enhances cold tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA1112100, PRJNA1112565; aroma metabolomics: MTBLS13072
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GMRN identified NtCYC as the top TF associated with DHA production. 2. NtCYC directly binds and activates the NtLOX2 promoter. 3. NtCYC overexpression increases DHA and β-ionone, whereas knockout decreases them. 4. NtCYC redirects aroma metabolic flux, reducing β-damascenone relative to other apocarotenoids. 5. NtCYC negatively affects cold tolerance through peroxide/lipid peroxidation-related mechanisms.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for overexpression; native promoter of NtLOX2 assayed in transient reporter system
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: carotenoid degradation-linked aroma biosynthesis; branch selection; flux rewiring
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes DHA and β-ionone accumulation while altering aroma profile
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increases MDA/lipid peroxidation; reduces cold tolerance; redirects flux away from β-damascenone
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: field-grown plants in high-altitude vs low-altitude regions; developmental time course after topping; cold stress at 10°C
